# Project State

## Project Reference

See: .planning/PROJECT.md (updated 2026-07-13)

**Core value:** After any natural pause in what you're saying or hearing, a relevant, streamed answer appears in the stealth overlay — generated by a local model from your screen + speech + your own notes — fast and private.
**Current focus:** Phase 4 — Continuous Hearing (Resident STT + Ambient Listening) — 🟢 CODE-COMPLETE (all 9 of 9 plans done: 04-01…04-09). 04-09 (cloud STT removal — the FINAL plan, STT-05/SC6) COMPLETE — Task 1 prove-then-remove gate human-approved at the orchestrator level (2026-07-16, "Approved — delete Azure") on the 04-08 keyless proof; Tasks 2–4 executed + committed atomically (explicit pathspec): b0780dc (feat: delete the ~380-line browser-DOM polyfill + SDK require + all cloud code paths in speech.service.js 1648→918L, drop speech.provider/speech.azure config, uninstall microsoft-cognitiveservices-speech-sdk +8 deps + refresh lockfile, keep node-record-lpcm16), dec5a55 (feat: collapse the speech UI + env — main.js getters/env, onboarding card/panel, settings dropdown+fields+dead Python CLI, first-run template+fields, env.example keys+stale Python seed — to the single local whisper engine), cd5b383 (refactor: retire ensureNativeGlobalURL — definition + export + 4 call sites incl. the undocumented whisper-server.manager.js one + 3 poison tests — while KEEPING nodeFetch). Gates: make run_tests 145/145, make lint 0, keyless LocalProvider wiring check passes, headless boot degrades-to-mic clean (windowCount 4, no uncaught). 3 Rule-3/1 auto-fixes (4th ensureNativeGlobalURL call site in whisper-server.manager.js; stale global.URL/polyfill comments in 4 files; unused fs require). STT-05/SC6 = DONE; STT collapses to the resident whisper.cpp engine, no provider selection/cloud UI/env/config. NEXT: Phase 4 → main merge (human performs merge+push; never auto), then post-merge memory capture + Phase 5. 8-plan history: 04-05 (system-audio tap) Tasks 1–3 shipped, Task 4 signing spike DEFERRED to Phase 8; 04-06 (ambient resilience) 145/145; 04-08 (validation gate) real-world run DEFERRED to pre-ship/Phase 8 (keyless proof accepted). Phase 3 COMPLETE & merged to main (commit 0a65a3a).

## Accumulated Context

### Decisions

Decisions are logged in PROJECT.md Key Decisions table.
Load-bearing sequencing decisions driving this roadmap:

- Phase 2→3: Abstraction lands FIRST (Gemini wrapped verbatim), Local is proven, Gemini + Azure removed LAST — never removal-first (Pitfall 12).
- Phase 1: One generic `ServiceSupervisor`, configured twice — must precede the model server (P3) and STT server (P4).
- Phase 5: DOMPurify (SEC-01) + macOS TCC recovery (SEC-02) ship in the SAME phase as continuous capture (CONT-04) / md-context (CONT-05) — the features that create the threat surface.
- Local runtime = Ollama ≥ 0.19 (`qwen3-vl:8b`), adopt-if-present / own-if-started; cache at `~/.ollama/models` (not `~/.cache`).

- Phase 3 (gap-closure during the 03-07 gate): fixed onboarding "Ollama not detected" (debug session `ollama-not-detected`, commit 2628f8a). Root cause: `LocalModelManager._probeVersion()` probed the daemon with the ambient global `fetch`, which in the ELECTRON MAIN process is Chromium-net-backed and false-negatives a loopback (127.0.0.1:11434) daemon that Node `http` reaches fine — so `getStatus().serverUp` was `false` (both onboarding labels + the Continue gate read that one boolean) even though the ServiceSupervisor's Node-`http` probe had already ADOPTED the same daemon (smoking-gun: one log line with `state:'adopted', serverUp:false`). Fix: `_probeVersion()` now probes `/api/version` via `ServiceSupervisor.probeHttp` (Node http), dropping the global-`fetch` dependency. TDD RED→GREEN (regression test #8: serverUp stays true when the daemon is HTTP-reachable but global fetch is broken); 84/84 tests, eslint clean. The earlier macOS-GUI-PATH hypothesis was ELIMINATED (the binary was resolved/adopted; the transport was the defect). **Durable lesson for Phase 4+ (whisper-server probing): never use the global `fetch` for loopback health probes in the Electron main process — use Node `http` (probeHttp).** Also captured (separate): onboarding-forced-Gemini-apikey gap fix (dcd8edb). RUNTIME-VERIFIED IN ELECTRON MAIN (post-first-pass): the fixed `_probeVersion` returns `serverUp:true` when run under the REAL electron binary (headless `electron` run), not just pure node — closing the first pass's verification gap. A user-reported "still not found" recurrence was a STALE long-lived macOS process (closing the window ≠ quitting → old require-cache serves pre-fix code; single-instance lock re-focuses it), NOT a code regression — resolved by a full process kill + relaunch. LESSON: on macOS/Electron, code changes require a full app QUIT (Cmd+Q / Ctrl+C the `npm start` terminal / `pkill -f "OpenCluely/node_modules/electron"`), never a window close-and-reopen. Separate follow-up spotted: `TypeError: Object has been destroyed` in onActivate (main.js:1534 — missing destroyed-window guard in windowManager.windows.forEach). DEEPER FIX (commit 7b4a48a — the transport fix alone was insufficient): the Azure STT polyfill (`speech.service.js`, required at main.js top) REPLACES `global.URL` process-wide with a fake that parses ANY host to localhost:443 and has no `searchParams` — breaking `_probeVersion` (probed localhost:443 → serverUp:false), OpenAI's internal `buildURL` (`Object.fromEntries(url.searchParams)` threw), and the ollama client's `formatHost`; its window/document/navigator triad also tripped OpenAI's browser guard so `new OpenAI()` threw "browser-like environment" (LLM never initialized). Fix: new `src/core/local-transport.js` (`ensureNativeGlobalURL()` restores native `node:url` URL; `nodeFetch()` = WHATWG fetch over Node http/https, immune to Electron-main Chromium-net loopback false-negatives). Manager: native URL in `_probeVersion`, `ensureNativeGlobalURL()` in ctor, `fetch:nodeFetch` on the ollama client, a `getStatus()` guard so a model-probe throw can't flip `serverUp`. LocalProvider: `ensureNativeGlobalURL()` + `dangerouslyAllowBrowser:true` + `fetch:nodeFetch`. Polyfill left intact (removal = Phase 4/STT-05). TDD RED→GREEN, 93/93 tests, eslint clean. VERIFIED under the REAL polyfill (speech.service loaded + live daemon): `getStatus().serverUp===true` + LocalProvider constructs. Human GUI relaunch still pending to confirm the app log shows serverUp:true + Step 3 unblocks + model answers. LESSON: the Azure browser-DOM polyfill globally pollutes window/URL — modern SDKs (openai/ollama) + native URL must be shielded until the Phase-4 STT rebuild removes the polyfill; this is RESEARCH Flag 4 biting in practice. STREAMING FOLLOW-ON FIX (commit "fix(03): guard nodeFetch stream close"): the nodeFetch fix made the LLM reachable but `Readable.toWeb(res)` double-closed the web-stream controller on end+close / reader.cancel → uncaught `ERR_INVALID_STATE` ("Controller is already closed") crashed the app mid-answer. Replaced with a hand-built ReadableStream (single guarded close) + a regression test exercising reader.cancel()+socket-close. 94/94 tests, eslint clean; VERIFIED end-to-end — a real streamed openai completion via nodeFetch (polyfill loaded, live daemon) returned with NO crash (the app-side local engine is now fully correct). SEPARATE ENV ISSUE (NOT our code): the user's running daemon is the Ollama.app (`/Applications/Ollama.app/Contents/Resources/ollama serve`), a broken/incomplete install MISSING its `llama-server` backend → `/api/generate` 500s ("llama-server binary not found") for ANY model, bypassing OpenCluely entirely (curl reproduces). The Homebrew ollama 0.32.0 IS complete (`/opt/homebrew/Cellar/ollama/0.32.0/libexec/lib/ollama/llama-server` present). Resolution for the human gate: quit the Ollama.app and run the Homebrew daemon (`ollama serve` / `brew services start ollama`) — same `~/.ollama` models, no re-pull. The 03-07 gate can complete once a working daemon serves.

- Phase 3: model-availability UX landed (plan 03-06, PROV-05) — pure renderer over the 03-04 IPC, no new IPC. Onboarding gained two always-on screens (Local is the default engine → core setup, not STT-gated): `data-screen="ollama"` guide-installs openwhispr-style (detect via `getModelStatus().serverUp`; missing → install guidance + `openExternal` ollama.com/download + Re-check; advancement BLOCKED until a running engine is found — never bundles/silently fails), and `data-screen="model-pull"` runs `modelPreflight()` (renders disk/RAM warnings, warn-never-block) then auto-`pullModel('qwen3-vl:8b')` with a real percent bar from `onModelPullProgress` (reuses the whisper download-progress plumbing; friendly-failure — Continue enables once the pull settles either way, partial pull resumes). STT onboarding intact; the Gemini `apikey` screen deliberately stays until PROV-07. Overlay (`src/ui/main-window.js`) gained `showLocalUnavailable(status)` — inline dismissible panel keyed strictly off `getStatus`: `owned&&!serverUp`→Restart (`recoverModel('restart')`); `adopted&&!serverUp`→Open Settings (NEVER restart a foreign daemon); `serverUp&&!modelPresent`→Re-download (`recoverModel('repull')` w/ inline progress); `!modelResponds`/OOM→Open Settings. Triggered from the 03-03 canned fallback (`metadata.usedFallback`) + any `llm-error`; overlay now subscribes to `llm-response`/`transcription-llm-response`/`llm-error` (via `broadcastToAllWindows`) and dismisses on a real answer. 2 in-scope Rule-3 adaptations (wired the overlay's dormant response/error handlers since rendering lives in out-of-scope chat-window.js; self-contained panel spinner since index.html ships no Font Awesome). WIRING-verified only — proven at 03-07. eslint 0, 83/83. Commits d2f2194/2136253/2315959.

- Phase 3: PROV-06 UI landed (plan 03-05). Settings gains an "AI Model" section: provider picker `#llmProvider` (Local default / Gemini "being removed"), curated `#localModel` (qwen3-vl:8b default, :30b, gemma3:4b/12b) + advanced `#localModelAdvanced` from `listInstalledModels()`, `#modelStatus` health line (adopted "Using your running Ollama" vs owned "Managed by OpenCluely" + serverUp/modelPresent/modelResponds from `getStatus()`), `testProviderConnection()` button, repair via `pullModel()` streaming `onModelPullProgress` into `install-log`. ZERO new IPC — reuses the 03-04 bridges. `getSettings` surfaces `provider`/`model`/`curatedModels`; `saveSettings` persists `LLM_PROVIDER` (local|gemini) + `LOCAL_MODEL` to `.env` via `persistEnvUpdates` → restart-to-apply (facade resolves provider at module load; no live hot-swap, matches stealth-name relaunch + speech-provider patterns). Gemini option + `geminiKey` retained until PROV-07. WIRING-verified only. eslint 0, 83/83. Commits 62e04e1/fac8b85/787d976/091740b.

- Phase 3: PROV-05 (LocalModelManager) landed (plan 03-04). First real `ServiceSupervisor` consumer — thin configurator, `adopt:true` + HTTP health on 11434, SIGTERM→SIGKILL on the owned child; NO new deps (consumes the 03-01 `ollama` client). `ensureModel`/`pullModel` = resumable sha256-verified pull emitting `{status,percent,completed,total}`; cache at Ollama default `~/.ollama/models`. `keep_alive:-1` belt-and-suspenders (`OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE` env when we spawn + an always-on `warmUp generate({keep_alive:-1})` covering an adopted daemon too). `getStatus` fuses owned-vs-adopted + three health fields (`serverUp`/`modelPresent`/`modelResponds`). `_ownsSupervisor` guard: the binary-not-found→not-installed check fires only for a self-built supervisor, so an injected supervisor is trusted (adopt/own provable network-free) while guide-install UX holds in prod. Lifecycle: lazy `getLocalModelManager()`, `start()` in `onAppReady` (isolated try/catch, never blocks startup), `stop()` fire-and-forget in `onWillQuit` (no-op if adopted). IPC is provider-neutral/local-named (`download-model`, `get-model-status`, `list-installed-models`, `model-preflight`, `recover-model`, `test-provider-connection`) + `model-pull-progress` stream + preload bridges — survives PROV-07; `recover-model 'restart'` only restarts a daemon we own. Whisper IPC untouched. 83/83, eslint 0. Commits ee0829f/e7b5fb0/448cfbc/01f0b18.

- Phase 3: LocalProvider landed (plan 03-03, PROV-03/PROV-04/SC4). `LocalProvider extends LLMProvider` mirrors GeminiProvider's full public surface so the facade re-export keeps every `main.js llmService.*` call-site unchanged. `serialize(neutral)` is the ONLY OpenAI wire-shape site: system prefix (`systemPrompt`+`mdContext`, mdContext wired but empty until Phase 5), neutral `'model'`→OpenAI `'assistant'` history rename, images as nested `{type:'image_url',image_url:{url:'data:<mime>;base64,<b64>'}}` parts (RESEARCH Flag 2, not the bare-string variant). Transport = `openai` SDK (`chat.completions.create`, `baseURL=host/v1`, `apiKey:'ollama'`); `keep_alive:-1` in the body as defense-in-depth (authoritative residency = 03-04 warm-up). Counters increment only in `process*` (not `generate`/`generateStream`) so a `process*→generateStream` delegation counts once. Registry flip: `selected = config.get('llm.provider')` (was hardcoded `'gemini'`) — Local is now the config-default engine, both `{gemini,local}` registered, `getSelected()` hardened never-undefined; Gemini selectable via `LLM_PROVIDER=gemini` until PROV-07. Network-free constructor (never throws Ollama-down). Live streaming round-trip against real Ollama deferred to 03-07. Commits 6e3b799/0d9a902/38cbb1a/acbe382.

- Phase 3: GEN-01 generalization landed (plan 03-02). Default skill = concise **General** reply-suggester; DSA retired as first-class and folded into an opt-in **Coding** skill (canonical `programming`). Shipped skills = General (default) + Coding only. Both prompt sources de-interviewed: the `.md` path (`prompt-loader` whitelists `['general','programming']`; new `prompts/general.md`; `dsa.md` deleted) and the hardcoded transcription prompt (`request-builder.js:35` interview line removed; rewritten short-by-default / expand-on-depth, H1 + code-fence preserved). `dsa`/`data-structures`/`algorithms` aliases fold to `programming` and the language-injection `case` fires for `programming`, so coding-optimality behavior + `getSkillPrompt('dsa')` still resolve. `main.js` default `activeSkill` `dsa`→`general`; settings picker + onboarding copy repositioned; transcription golden regenerated (retires at PROV-07). 63/63, eslint 0. Commits 22cae12/b5fe940/1147042/5539f09. NOTE: FUNCTIONAL scrub only — residual copy-only `'dsa'` strings remain elsewhere (see Blockers → Deferred), for README P8 / website P9.

- Phase 3: PROV-06 foundation (config half) landed (plan 03-01). Two CJS-safe transport deps pinned — `openai ^6.47.0` (LocalProvider `/v1`, 03-03) + `ollama ^0.6.3` (LocalModelManager lifecycle, 03-04), both `require()`-safe. `config.llm` restructured to `{ provider, local, gemini }`: `provider = LLM_PROVIDER || 'local'`; `local = { host: OLLAMA_BASE_URL||'http://127.0.0.1:11434', model: LOCAL_MODEL||'qwen3-vl:8b', keepAlive:-1, curatedModels:[qwen3-vl:8b/30b, gemma3:4b/12b] }`. Registry deliberately NOT flipped — `providers.getSelected()` still resolves to `GeminiProvider`, so the app stays on the proven Gemini path until 03-03 registers Local (honors never-removal-first). `llm.gemini` preserved byte-for-byte (Phase-2 goldens still 63/63); deleted only at PROV-07 (03-08). `speech.*` untouched (Azure STT → Phase 4). Commits 794ce1f (deps), 0b2cea6 (config).

- Phase 2: Seam COMPLETED — thin facade + provider-owned cert/UA (plan 02-03, SC1/SC2/SC3). `src/services/llm.service.js` is now a 17-line thin facade — `const providers = require('./providers'); module.exports = providers.getSelected();` — re-exporting the registry-selected provider instance; the 1654-line Gemini god-file body was deleted (it had been relocated to the provider in 02-02). Because the provider preserves the identical method surface (all 9 methods main.js calls) and the same `{ response, metadata }` returns, all 11 `llmService.*` call-sites in main.js are byte-for-byte unchanged (SC1). SAFE-FORM (deviation from the plan's illustrative snippet): getSelected() reads `this.providers[this.selected]`, so the facade calls it ON the registry object — NOT the plan's destructured `const { getSelected } = ...; getSelected()`, which would drop `this` and throw. Gemini's cert-verify bypass + UA override (exact UA string, callback(0) trust / callback(-2) default, hostname-guarded to generativelanguage.googleapis.com) relocated VERBATIM from unconditional global startup into `GeminiProvider.configureNetworkSession(ses)` (gemini.provider.js:239); `main.js setupNetworkConfiguration()` (line 289, invoked 223) now delegates to `require('./src/services/providers').getSelected()` gated on `configureNetworkSession` existing (SC3) — the bypass is no longer inline and vanishes cleanly when Gemini is removed in Phase 3 (a keyless Local provider that omits the method drops it automatically; it is NOT part of the 4-method interface). Azure/STT isolation held: speech.service.js:5 (userAgent) + main-window.js:653-656 (userAgentData) untouched, in neither 02-03 commit. Task-3 live-Gemini smoke WAIVED — no GEMINI_API_KEY (moving off cloud is the project's point), so the live transport path is unrunnable here and never worked in this env; substituted no-key runtime verification (facade loads as GeminiProvider with all 9 methods; graceful "Gemini API key not configured" degradation, no throw; SC3 wiring trace live) + automated parity (eslint 0; 63/63 incl. byte-identical goldens across generate + generateStream; SC1 zero call-site changes). Transport relocated verbatim → the one unverified bit (live SSE round-trip) is unchanged code fed a byte-identical request; true e2e deferred to keyless Phase 3. 2 atomic commits: 95a9936 (facade, 1655−/17+), 47ecfdf (cert/UA relocation).

- Phase 2: Gemini provider + byte-identical parity landed (plan 02-02, SC2/SC3/SC4). `GeminiProvider extends LLMProvider` (`src/services/providers/gemini.provider.js`) relocates the Gemini transport/client/retry/error/fallback/testConnection logic VERBATIM out of the 1654-line `llm.service.js` (only require paths + `extends`/`super()` changed). `serialize(neutral)` is the SINGLE neutral→Gemini wire mapper — key insertion order `contents` → `generationConfig` → `systemInstruction` reproduces today's exact object so JSON.stringify is byte-identical; no Gemini shape leaks into RequestBuilder (SC4). The 6 process* methods stay verbatim except the request-build call, now `serialize(requestBuilder.build*Request(...))`; `generate`/`generateStream` are thin siblings (serialize + execute*/executeStreamingRequest, returning answer TEXT, honoring `options.programmingLanguage`); `isAvailable` returns `isInitialized`. The provider imports NEITHER sessionManager NOR promptLoader (RequestBuilder owns them; the deleted build*/format*/getIntelligentTranscriptionPrompt methods were their only users). Registry (`src/services/providers/index.js`) is `{ providers, selected:'gemini', register, get, getSelected }` exported as the object — hardcoded default, no user switch this phase (Phase 3 adds it; SC3 anchor for cert/UA gating in 02-03). Parity pinned by 3 goldens captured from the LIVE llm.service (transport monkeypatched, network-free) via `scripts/capture-gemini-goldens.js` (exports shared FIXED inputs + fakes, guarded by `require.main` so the test imports them side-effect-free); `test/gemini-request-parity.test.js` asserts exact string equality for text/image/transcription AND that generate + generateStream build the identical golden. God-files untouched; whole-repo eslint + 63/63 node:test green.

- Phase 2: Abstraction skeleton landed FIRST (plan 02-01, PROV-02/SC2/SC4 groundwork). `LLMProvider` base contract (`src/services/providers/llm-provider.js`) names exactly generate/generateStream/isAvailable/testConnection, each throwing by default (subclass-must-override). `RequestBuilder` (`src/core/request-builder.js`) is a pure DI module (`constructor({ sessionManager, promptLoader })` defaulting to the real singletons — the Phase-1 service-supervisor DI shape) that emits ONE input-neutral struct `{ kind, skill, systemPrompt, userText, images[], history[], mdContext }` for text/image/transcription — NO Gemini wire keys (contents/parts/systemInstruction/generationConfig), those stay for the provider's serialize step (02-02). Prompt helpers (formatUserMessage/formatImageInstruction/getIntelligentTranscriptionPrompt) relocated byte-identical (verified by a line-range source-slice → runtime diff harness, 432 assertions across 4 skills x 9 langs); history limits preserved exactly (text 15; transcription 10-then-last-8) with system/empty filtering + `model→model / *→user` mapping. No `programmingLanguage` field on the struct (it drives assembly via the helpers; response-side enforcement is the provider's job). `buildImageRequest` takes a Buffer (`.toString('base64')`) or a base64 string. God-files (llm.service.js/main.js/session.manager.js) untouched — purely additive; whole-repo eslint + 56/56 node:test green.

- Phase 1: VAD decision logic extracted to a pure `src/core/vad-segmenter.js` (action-return delegation: accumulate|flush|discard|noop); `SpeechService` keeps buffer storage/Whisper flush and delegates the VAD-enabled path — established the pure-logic extraction + node:test pattern (FND-01, plan 01-02).

- Phase 1: `.env` string round-trip (`src/core/env-file.js`: parseEnv/formatEnvValue/upsertEnvContent) and skill/prompt normalization (`src/core/skill-normalizer.js`) extracted as pure fs-free modules; `main.js`/`first-run.js`/`prompt-loader.js` delegate with zero behavior change. Tests require only the extracted modules (quality gate); delegating files import only the symbols they use to stay lint-clean for 01-04 (FND-01, plan 01-01).

- Phase 1: Generic `ServiceSupervisor` (`src/core/service-supervisor.js`, FND-04) built once with a DI spawn seam (`options.spawn || spawn`), port/HTTP health probes, capped exponential backoff → give-up 'failed', SIGTERM→SIGKILL (awaits reaping), and adopt-if-present/own-if-started (stop() never kills a foreign process). Hardened startup uses a one-shot `_startupSettled` guard to prevent double-restart/hang; stop() settles immediately if the owned child already exited. Fits both future consumers unchanged (Ollama HTTP+adopt P3, whisper-server TCP+own+pidFile P4). Proven by a real-spawn node:test suite (SC3/SC4), plan 01-03.

- Phase 1: ESLint 9 flat config (`eslint.config.js`, FND-02 lint half) is the whole-repo gate — lean error-only ruleset (`no-undef` + lenient `no-unused-vars` with `caughtErrors:'none'`), per-env files blocks (Node/CommonJS vs renderer `script` with browser + app-injected globals incl. `require` for chat-window dual-load), vendored/standalone dirs ignored but hand-written `lib/mathrender.js` linted. `eslint@^9` + `globals` are committed devDeps with a synced lockfile (npm-ci parity). `npx eslint .` is the exact gate 01-05's Makefile/CI will invoke; repo made green surgically (dead-code removal + `_`-prefixed unused args + removed stale disable directives), no mass reformat (plan 01-04).

### Pending Todos

[From .planning/todos/pending/ — ideas captured during sessions]

- **Natural-pause detection → match OpenWhispr's method (Phase 4 / STT-04–05).** Today the local mic path uses a hand-rolled energy-RMS VAD (`src/core/vad-segmenter.js`: adaptive noise floor + hysteresis + silence-hangover + pre-roll ring), and the Azure path uses Azure's own endpointing (`EndSilenceTimeoutMs`/`Speech_SegmentationSilenceTimeoutMs`=2000). OpenWhispr instead runs a **neural Silero VAD inside whisper.cpp's `whisper-server`** (`--vad --vad-model --vad-threshold --vad-min-speech-duration-ms --vad-min-silence-duration-ms --vad-max-speech-duration-s --vad-speech-pad-ms`). When Phase 4 replaces the STT layer with a supervised resident `whisper-server` (the OPENWHISPR-NOTES §5 recommendation), turn on its `--vad` Silero option so pause detection matches OpenWhispr; our current tunables (silenceHangoverMs / minUtteranceMs / maxUtteranceMs / preRollMs) map 1:1 onto those flags. — surfaced 2026-07-14 during the 03-07 gate.
- **Auto-answer-on-natural-pause = the continuous flow (Phase 5/6), not Phase 3.** The "after any natural pause a relevant answer appears" core value = continuous capture (CONT-04) + md-context (CONT-05) + the relevance-gate (Phase 6: thresholds/cooldown/self-speech suppression). Phase 3's three entry points are on-demand (type / screenshot / ask) by design — the pause→auto-trigger lands later. — surfaced 2026-07-14.

- **Local-Whisper STT onboarding is fragile on a broken system Python (Phase 4 fixes it).** The current Local-Whisper download spawns a system/pyenv Python whose `hashlib` must provide blake2; on a mis-built pyenv (seen: 3.11.12 without working OpenSSL/`_hashlib`) it dumps a raw `ValueError: unsupported hash type blake2b` traceback, and the onboarding whisper/model-download screen has no friendly skip/continue. Workaround: pick "Skip for now" on the speech screen (bypasses whisper entirely; STT is not needed for the Local-LLM 03-07 gate, and Azure STT stays available until Phase 4). Phase 4 (STT-04/05) replaces this whole Python path with a supervised resident whisper.cpp binary (no system Python) — the fragility disappears; also add friendly-failure + a skip affordance on the whisper download screen then. — surfaced 2026-07-14 during the 03-07 gate.

- **Harden Ollama adoption against a broken/backend-less daemon (surfaced 2026-07-14, 03-07 gate).** The app IS designed to manage Ollama (LocalModelManager/ServiceSupervisor adopt-if-present / own-if-started + keep-resident + stop-on-quit) — normal users never run `ollama serve` by hand. But on a machine with TWO Ollamas (a broken **Ollama.app** missing its `llama-server` backend + a complete Homebrew ollama), the broken app auto-ran a daemon that PASSED the `serverUp` health probe (so onboarding — which gates only on `serverUp` — let the user through) yet 500'd on every inference (`llama-server binary not found`; `modelResponds:false`). Hardening: (1) onboarding/detection + the Local-down recovery (03-06) should weight `modelResponds` (the 3-level health already computes it), not just `serverUp`, so a "daemon up but can't answer" install is flagged; (2) `_resolveOllamaBin` should prefer a COMPLETE install (or validate the llama-server backend) rather than potentially adopting/spawning a broken one. Not a code defect in the happy path (single clean install) — an edge case a dual-install exposed. Candidate for a Phase-3 gap-closure or Phase-6 robustness pass.

- **✅ FIXED 2026-07-15 (commit 25b7278). Onboarding "Probing" hang = the status health-check ran a full model generate (03-07 gate).** Fix: `_modelResponds` is now a cheap bounded ping (`think:false` + `options.num_predict:1` + hard timeout ≤2500ms via Promise.race), so a cold/stalled generate resolves false instead of hanging tens of seconds; `getStatus({ probeResponds:false })` skips the responds probe entirely on the serverUp-gated onboarding detect (opts threaded preload→IPC→`onboarding.runOllamaDetect`). Settings/recovery/test-connection keep the default full probe. TDD RED→GREEN tests 11-13; 102/102, eslint 0. Original diagnosis: With the Homebrew daemon healthy (serverUp + /api/tags OK, model pulled), `getStatus()` STILL blocks because `_modelResponds()` does `ollama.generate({ prompt:'', keep_alive })` — a real generation that cold-loads the 10 GB model AND (no /no_think on this native-API call) can trigger qwen3's verbose thinking, so onboarding sits on "Probing" for tens of seconds per probe. A `think:false + num_predict:1` liveness test still exceeded 20s (cold-load likely dominates; also confirm whether native `think:false` is honored on qwen3-vl). Fix direction (small, next session): (1) make `_modelResponds`/`warmUp` a CHEAP ping — `think:false` + `options.num_predict:1` + a hard timeout; (2) do NOT block the serverUp-gated onboarding Continue on `modelResponds` (probe it async / drop it from the gate — serverUp + modelPresent is enough to proceed). Complements the earlier "weight modelResponds but never hang detection" hardening note.

- **✅ HARDENED 2026-07-15 (commit 27d8737). App auto-start Ollama — own-if-started binary-resolution + spawn PATH.** Fix: `_resolveOllamaBin` fallbacks now also cover `~/.ollama/bin/ollama`, and the spawn `def.env.PATH` prepends the standard bin dirs (`/opt/homebrew/bin`, `/usr/local/bin`, `~/.ollama/bin`, the resolved binary's dir) while folding `process.env.PATH` back in (the supervisor merges def.env over process.env, which would otherwise DROP the inherited PATH). TDD RED→GREEN tests 14-15. **NUANCE found while fixing (be honest at the gate):** `/opt/homebrew/bin/ollama` was ALREADY in the darwin fallback list before this fix, and `_resolveOllamaBin` uses PATH-independent `fs.existsSync` — so on this machine (Homebrew ollama 0.32.0 present at `/opt/homebrew/bin/ollama`) the binary was ALREADY resolvable. If the app still "won't start itself," the cause is the spawn ENV (now PATH-hardened) or something other than binary resolution — the 03-07 gate confirms. Most reliable gate path: start the Homebrew daemon so the app ADOPTS it. Original diagnosis: `start()` spawns `ollama serve` only when `_resolveOllamaBin()` finds the binary; under Electron's stripped GUI PATH (no `/opt/homebrew/bin`), and after the broken `.app` + its `/usr/local/bin/ollama` symlink were removed, `_resolveOllamaBin` can't locate the Homebrew binary → `this._ownsSupervisor && !this.ollamaBin` surfaces "not-installed" and it NEVER spawns. (Adopt of an already-running daemon works fine — this only bites the no-daemon path.) Fix (small, next session): `_resolveOllamaBin` must existsSync standard macOS locations — `/opt/homebrew/bin/ollama`, `/usr/local/bin/ollama`, `~/.ollama/bin/ollama`, `/Applications/Ollama.app/Contents/Resources/ollama` — in addition to which/where, AND prepend those bin dirs to the ServiceSupervisor spawn `env.PATH`. Pairs with the health-check + adoption-hardening todos above.

### Blockers/Concerns

- **⚠️ qwen3-vl:8b over-reasons on heavy prompts even with /no_think → time-to-first-content latency concern (03-07 gate env-prep, 2026-07-15).** Env is READY: Homebrew daemon up (0.32.0), `qwen3-vl:8b` resident, `ollama ps` 100% GPU, keep_alive. The app's local answer path is PROVEN clean on simple prompts (direct openai-SDK /v1 probes: text ~4s TTFT, image ~5.6s, correct screenshot description, NO `<think>` leak). BUT on the realistic bounded-notes(12KB)+image prompt, qwen3-vl emits HUNDREDS of `<think>` tokens before content even with `/no_think` in the system prompt — the API `think:false` param is ignored, and the `/no_think` soft-switch only fully works on simple prompts. Effect: prefill ~6.8s PLUS a long reasoning phase before the first CONTENT token → time-to-first-answer well over the rough ~3-4s gate (empty at 256 tokens; content appears ~512). This is RESEARCH Flag 1's empirical TTFT question, now observed. DECISION (at/after the human gate — a 03-07 failure loops to gap closure, NEVER to removal): (1) accept it (rough gate is lenient; full sustained validation is Phase 6); (2) switch the default to a NON-reasoning curated model (gemma3:4b/12b) — no `<think>` phase, faster first token; (3) more forcefully disable qwen3-vl thinking (LOCAL_THINK is already off; investigate an Ollama/model update honoring think:false, or a Modelfile with the reasoning template stripped). Smoke hardened to surface this (commit db71009): answer measured over /v1 + /no_think, `SMOKE_NUM_PREDICT` tunable. My earlier "empty answer" was a smoke artifact (no /no_think + 64-cap + native /api/chat), NOT an app defect.

Research flags to resolve during planning (not blockers to starting):

- Phase 3: Empirical TTFT/memory validation of `qwen3-vl:8b` on 32 GB Apple Silicon (budget at session end + minute-45 pressure, not fresh-demo); multimodal request shape; Ollama adopt/own lifecycle.
- Phase 4: STT embedding sub-decision — in-process `smart-whisper` vs supervised `whisper-server`; validate native-addon ABI against Electron 29 early.
- Phase 6: Relevance-gate tuning (thresholds, cooldown, suppressing the assistant's own speech / hallucinated silence) — empirical, iterate in real use.

Deferred (captured, not blocking):

- GEN-01 residual copy (from 03-02): copy-only `'dsa'` skill defaults/labels/emoji maps still in `src/managers/session.manager.js:11`, `src/ui/main-window.js` (13/28/294/522/761/874), `src/ui/chat-window.js:301`, `src/services/providers/gemini.provider.js` (1166/1190 + comment 271 — removed anyway at PROV-07/03-08). Not functional; part of the in-app-string reposition stream slated for README P8 / website WEB-01 P9.
